title: 30 Best High-Protein Recipes (Easy Low-Calorie Meals) | Eati
introduction: High-protein meals make it easier to stay full, support muscle, and keep your calorie deficit on track. Below are 30 easy high-protein recipe ideas with nutrition snapshots and photos. Each recipe links to a detailed article on Eati.

sections.content:
Photo: /images/blog/crockpot-marry-me-chicken-253a15e4.png

Calories: 585 kcal
Fat: 37.8g
Protein: 47.6g
Ingredients: chicken breast, garlic, sun-dried tomatoes, parmesan, cream, chicken stock, spinach

Description: Slow-cooked flavor with a creamy, protein-forward sauce that stays satisfying even during fat loss.

sections.heading: How to Plan a Weekly High-Protein Meal Rotation
sections.content:
The biggest reason meal prep fails is variety burnout: people try to cook 15 different meals in a week and end up eating takeout by Thursday. A smarter approach is a simple rotation.

The 2-2-2 method:
• 2 breakfasts: Pick one savory (eggs + veggies) and one sweet (Greek yogurt + berries + oats). Swap between them daily.
• 2 lunches: Pick one protein + rice bowl and one protein + salad. Both can use the same prepped protein (e.g., baked chicken) with different sides.
• 2 dinners: Pick one quick weeknight option (like [Salsa Verde Chicken](/blog/salsa-verde-chicken-recipe) or [Sheet Pan Honey Garlic Chicken](/blog/honey-garlic-baked-chicken-and-cauliflower-recipe)) and one slow-cooker or batch-cook recipe (like [Crockpot Tuscan Chicken](/blog/crockpot-tuscan-chicken-recipe) or [Slow Cooker Chili](/blog/slow-cooker-chili-beef-and-pinto-beans-recipe)).

Cook 2–3 proteins in bulk on Sunday (e.g., 2 lbs chicken breast + 1 lb ground turkey), then portion into containers with different vegetables and carbs. This gives you 10+ meals with minimal repeat fatigue.

Use a calorie calculator to set your daily target and the protein calculator to verify each meal hits 30–40g protein. For more on why tracking helps, see [why counting calories actually works](/blog/why-counting-calories-actually-works).

sections.content:

sections.heading: High-Protein Meal Prep: Save Time and Money
sections.content:
Cooking these recipes in larger batches saves both time and money. Here's how to batch-prep like a pro:

Buy in bulk:
• Chicken breast is cheapest in family packs (often 40% less per pound).
• Ground turkey, ground beef (93/7), and cottage cheese freeze well.
• Frozen shrimp and salmon are usually cheaper per pound than fresh and nutritionally identical.

Cook once, eat 4 times:
• A single 3 lb batch of [Crockpot Tuscan Chicken](/blog/crockpot-tuscan-chicken-recipe) or [Slow Cooker Chili](/blog/slow-cooker-chili-beef-and-pinto-beans-recipe) yields 6–8 servings.
• [Baked Crack Chicken](/blog/baked-crack-chicken-recipe) reheats beautifully for 4 days in the fridge.
• Sheet-pan recipes are perfect because they cook protein + veggies at once.

Smart storage:
• Glass containers with dividers keep portions separate and logged easily.
• Label with cook date; most prepared meals keep 3–4 days in the fridge, 2 months in the freezer.
• Portion protein into pre-weighed bags to make macro-tracking automatic.

Read more about efficient food tracking in [how to track calories correctly](/blog/how-to-track-calories-correctly).

sections.heading: Choosing the Right Recipe for Your Goal
sections.content:
Not every high-protein recipe is ideal for every goal. Here's a quick decision framework:

Goal: Fast fat loss with minimal cooking
→ Pick: 3-ingredient sheet pan + slow cooker recipes under 400 cal. Examples: [Salsa Verde Chicken](/blog/salsa-verde-chicken-recipe), [Hawaiian Chicken Sheet Pan](/blog/hawaiian-chicken-sheet-pan-recipe), [Slow Cooker Chicken Tortilla Soup](/blog/slow-cooker-chicken-tortilla-soup-recipe).

Goal: Balanced fat loss (moderate deficit)
→ Pick: 400–550 cal meals with 40g+ protein. Examples: [Fajita Chicken Casserole](/blog/fajita-chicken-casserole-recipe), [Chicken Mozzarella Bake](/blog/chicken-mozzarella-bake-recipe), [Baked Crack Chicken](/blog/baked-crack-chicken-recipe).

Goal: Comfort food without guilt
→ Pick: creamy/cheesy recipes with strong protein. Examples: [Crockpot Marry Me Chicken](/blog/crockpot-marry-me-chicken-recipe), [Cheesy French Onion Chicken](/blog/cheesy-french-onion-chicken-recipe), [Baked Pesto Mozzarella Chicken](/blog/baked-pesto-mozzarella-chicken-recipe).

Goal: Muscle gain / maintenance
→ Pick: 550–700+ cal recipes with higher carbs. Examples: [Creamy Sun-Dried Tomato Orzo](/blog/creamy-sun-dried-tomato-chicken-orzo-recipe), [Creamy Cottage Cheese Pasta](/blog/creamy-cottage-cheese-pasta-recipe), [Garlic Chicken and Potatoes](/blog/garlic-chicken-and-potatoes-skillet-recipe).

For a deeper understanding of your calorie and protein targets, see [high-protein low-calorie foods](/blog/high-protein-low-calorie-foods) and [best foods for weight loss](/blog/best-foods-for-weight-loss).

midArticleCta: Want faster results? Log one of these meals in Eati and use the meal breakdown to fine-tune your portions for a higher-protein, lower-calorie plan.
conclusion: The easiest way to eat more protein is to make it enjoyable and repeatable. Choose 3 to 5 favorites, rotate them for meal prep, and let Eati help you track your macros so progress stays consistent.
metaTitle: 30 Best High-Protein Recipes: Easy Low-Calorie Meals with Full Macros
metaDescription: 30 easy high-protein recipes under 700 cal with full nutrition breakdowns. Browse by calorie range, get meal prep tips, and pick the right recipe for fat loss, muscle gain, or comfort food.

faqs.question: How many grams of protein should I aim for to lose fat?
faqs.answer: A common starting point is 1.6–2.2 g of protein per kg of body weight per day. Use the Protein Calculator on Eati to tailor a target to your weight and goal.

faqs.question: Are high-protein recipes always low in calories?
faqs.answer: Not automatically. Many high-protein meals can be calorie-dense depending on portion sizes and sauces. The solution is to choose lean protein, control portions, and verify with the Meal Log Calculator.

faqs.question: What are the easiest high-protein meals for meal prep?
faqs.answer: Slow cooker soups, casseroles, and sheet pan dinners are typically the most meal-prep-friendly. They hold well, require less active cooking, and make logging macros faster in Eati.

faqs.question: Can I hit 40g+ protein in a single meal?
faqs.answer: Yes—many of these recipes are designed around protein-rich ingredients. If you’re targeting 40g+ in one sitting, track servings in Eati and adjust with the Meal Log Calculator until it fits your calorie deficit.
